title: Capability acquisition packet — the gate-enforced per-build loadout review
blocking_rules: a required capability packet missing -> gate blocks
missing_capabilities non-empty but presented_to_claude false -> gate blocks (no model silently proceeds with a weaker setup)
must_request_user_or_install non-empty -> blocked until the user completes setup or the architecture is changed to avoid the dependency
relation_to_task_loadouts: This packet is the ENFORCED form of a loadout review — attached to a dossier via --capabilities, validated by the gate. The per-task reviews in ../TASK-LOADOUTS/ are the ADVISORY planning form for the pending Clotho slices; a slice that needs new external tools should graduate its review into capability packets on the slice dossier.
why: A loadout review that nothing enforces decays into a wish list. The packet form is fail-closed: an agent that needs a tool it does not have must surface that need and block until it is resolved — never quietly degrade.
change_rule: Field or blocking-rule changes route through the contract doc + gate change together (docs/institutional-memory/CHANGE-PROTOCOL.md); this contract must be updated in the same change or verify-contracts fails.

oracle.source_of_truth: build-gate/gate.mjs capability validation + the worked fixtures
oracle.test: build-gate/scripts/test-gate.mjs + build-gate/scripts/test-trust.mjs (capability packet paths); build-gate/examples/capability-blocked/ (fail-closed worked example) + examples/prototype-pass/capabilities/ (passing example)
oracle.contract_equals_code: docs/institutional-memory/verify-contracts.mjs (field set deep-equals the committed worked example packet; the fail-closed example fixtures exist)
